title: Enable Trust Flush by default for expired delegations
description:
# GitHub Issue #286: Make Trust Flush active by default so expired trust delegations are cleaned up without explicit opt-in

**Category**: enhancement | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: Flip the default semantics of `spec.trustFlush` from *opt-in* to *active by default* so that a freshly bootstrapped Keystone CR cleans up expired `trust` delegations from day one, without the operator having to remember to add a `trustFlush:` block to their CR. Today `spec.trustFlush` is a nil-able pointer (`*TrustFlushSpec` at `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:75-79`), and the reconciler explicitly skips CronJob creation when the pointer is nil — `reconcile_trustflush.go:34-47` sets `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired, message: \"Trust flush is not configured\"` and returns. As a result, every Keystone CR that does not include `spec.trustFlush` accumulates expired trust rows in the `trust` table indefinitely, growing the table monotonically and degrading queries that scan it (`keystone-manage trust_flush` is the only supported way to GC that table — Keystone does not delete rows on token expiry).

The CRD already declares the right defaults at the leaf level: `Schedule` carries `+kubebuilder:default=\"0 * * * *\"` (`keystone_types.go:340-342`) and `Suspend` carries `+kubebuilder:default=false` (`keystone_types.go:344-346`). Those defaults only fire once the parent struct is materialized. Today nothing materializes it on the unset path, so the leaf defaults are dead code for any CR that omits the field. This issue closes that gap by having the defaulting webhook materialize an empty `TrustFlushSpec` when the pointer is nil — the CRD-layer leaf defaults then fill in `schedule`/`suspend`, and the reconciler's existing Path 1 (`reconcile_trustflush.go:49-61`) takes over and creates the CronJob with the documented hourly schedule.

Concrete steps:

1. Add a defaulting block to `KeystoneWebhook.Default()` at `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:80-132`, alongside the existing `Replicas`, `Bootstrap.AdminUser`, `Cache.Backend` defaults:
   ```go
   if obj.Spec.TrustFlush == nil {
       obj.Spec.TrustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{
           Schedule: \"0 * * * *\",
           Suspend:  false,
       }
   }
   ```
   Setting `Schedule` and `Suspend` explicitly here avoids relying on the CRD-layer marker firing in the same admission round-trip — the webhook is the single source of truth, mirroring the `Replicas = 3` / `Bootstrap.Region = \"RegionOne\"` style at `keystone_webhook.go:81-98`. The `+kubebuilder:default` markers stay in place as a defense-in-depth fallback for callers that bypass the webhook (e.g. envtest without the defaulting webhook wired up).
2. Update the field godoc on `KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush` (`keystone_types.go:75-79`) and on `TrustFlushSpec` (`keystone_types.go:335-339`) to reflect the new posture: *\"When unset, the defaulting webhook materializes a populated TrustFlushSpec so the operator runs `keystone-manage trust_flush` hourly by default. To pause trust flush without removing the CronJob, set `suspend: true`. There is no nil-back path — admission re-defaults the pointer.\"*
3. Drop the `Path 2` branch from `reconcile_trustflush.go:34-47` — once the webhook always materializes the pointer, `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` is unreachable in normal operation. Keep `deleteCronJob()` itself for finalizer/garbage-collection paths and for envtest/unit-test scenarios that construct CRs without the webhook in the loop. Replace the inline nil-branch with a one-line guard that defends against the bypass case (envtest, kubectl-without-admission-webhook): if `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil`, log a warning and treat it as the (now legacy) opt-out path so the controller does not panic.
4. Decide what `TrustFlushReady` should report when the new default is active. Today the two outcomes are `TrustFlushReady` (CronJob present) and `TrustFlushNotRequired` (CronJob absent). After the flip, the latter only fires on the legacy bypass path. Recommendation: keep both reasons (the constant strings are referenced by `reconcile_trustflush_test.go:104,182,230` and by `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/chainsaw-test.yaml`) and document that `TrustFlushNotRequired` is now a legacy bypass-only signal. Do **not** introduce a new `TrustFlushSuspended` reason — `suspend: true` keeps the CronJob and the reason should remain `TrustFlushReady`, mirroring how an Autoscaling-suspended HPA still reports `AutoscalingReady`.
5. Update the integration tests:
   - `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush_test.go` — the three `TrustFlushNil_*` cases (`TestReconcileTrustFlush_TrustFlushNil_NoExistingCronJob_SetsTrustFlushNotRequired` at `:168`, plus the deletion-path test at `:226`) construct the CR by hand and bypass the webhook. They will keep working as-is and continue to exercise the legacy bypass branch from step 3 — annotate them with a comment noting the bypass posture so future readers do not reintroduce the now-deleted production code path.
   - `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go` and `integration_test.go` — any test that asserts on `TrustFlushReady` reason needs a one-line audit. Cases that go through the webhook (admission-enabled envtest fixtures) will start reporting `TrustFlushReady` instead of `TrustFlushNotRequired`. Update assertions accordingly.
   - `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go` — add a `TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` case mirroring the existing `Replicas`/`AdminUser` defaulting tests, asserting that `spec.trustFlush` is non-nil with `schedule == \"0 * * * *\"` and `suspend == false` after `Default()`.
6. Update the e2e fixture at `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/00-keystone-cr.yaml:30-32`. The explicit `trustFlush:` block remains valid and exercises the user-set path. Add a sibling fixture (e.g. `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/00-keystone-cr.yaml`) that omits `spec.trustFlush` entirely and asserts (a) the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is created with `spec.schedule == \"0 * * * *\"`, (b) `TrustFlushReady=True` with reason `TrustFlushReady`, and (c) the `keystone` CR's serialized spec at the API server contains the materialized `trustFlush:` block (`kubectl get keystone -o yaml`). The existing `01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml:12` patches `trustFlush: null` to disable — after the flip, the webhook re-defaults that to a populated struct on the next admission. Replace that patch with `trustFlush: {suspend: true}` and update the matching assertions in `chainsaw-test.yaml:136+` to expect `TrustFlushReady` (not `TrustFlushNotRequired`) plus a CronJob with `spec.suspend == true` and zero recent jobs. Document the rationale in the chainsaw test header.
7. Update the docs:
   - `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:144,464-468` — change the row description from *\"When set, the operator creates a CronJob…\"* to *\"Defaults to a populated struct (hourly schedule). To pause without removing the CronJob, set `suspend: true`. The field cannot be unset back to nil — admission re-defaults it.\"* Update the `TrustFlushSpec` section narrative at `:461-468` and the CronJob mapping table accordingly.
   - `docs/quick-start.md:589` — change *\"Trust-flush CronJob created (if `spec.trustFlush` is set)\"* to *\"Trust-flush CronJob created — defaults to hourly\"*.
   - `docs/guides/observability.md:60` — same edit as the Quick Start row.
   - `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md:192` — note the new default-on posture and link to `suspend: true` as the documented way to pause.
   - `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:280,2406-2418,2628` — adjust the sub-reconciler description so the *Path 1*/*Path 2* split is described as legacy-bypass-only.
8. Document the **breaking-on-upgrade** behavior in `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md`. Existing CRs that omit `spec.trustFlush` on a cluster running the old operator version will get a populated `spec.trustFlush` materialized at the next admission round-trip after the operator upgrade (any reconcile that round-trips the CR through the API server, plus any user-issued `kubectl apply`/`kubectl edit`). On the next reconcile after that, the operator creates the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob, and within the configured interval the first `keystone-manage trust_flush` Job fires. The first run can be expensive on long-lived clusters that have never run trust flush — the trust table may contain millions of expired rows. Add a one-line callout that recommends running `keystone-manage trust_flush --date <recent>` manually once before the upgrade, or staggering the rollout, on clusters where the trust table is known to be very large.

**Motivation**: Three drivers, in order of weight.

(a) **Operationally correct default.** Trust delegations expire by `expires_at` but Keystone never garbage-collects the rows. Without `keystone-manage trust_flush` running on a schedule, the `trust` table grows unbounded for the lifetime of the deployment. Every Keystone deployment in production needs trust flush enabled; making it opt-in pushes that decision to every operator individually, and the silent-degradation failure mode (slow `trust` queries and growing DB size months after first install) is exactly the kind of thing CRD defaults exist to prevent. The leaf-level kubebuilder defaults on `Schedule` and `Suspend` show the original intent was already a sensible default; the only thing missing is materialization of the parent struct.

(b) **Symmetry with `credentialKeys`.** `CredentialKeysSpec` is a non-pointer field (`keystone_types.go:73`) with `+kubebuilder:default=\"0 0 * * 0\"` on `RotationSchedule` and `+kubebuilder:default=3` on `MaxActiveKeys`. A CR that omits `credentialKeys` still gets credential-key rotation on a weekly schedule because the CRD-layer defaults fire on the materialized non-nil struct. Trust flush is conceptually the same kind of *housekeeping CronJob the operator owns* and should default on for the same reason. The asymmetry today (`fernet` and `credentialKeys` default-on, `trustFlush` default-off) is historical, not principled.

(c) **The current `TrustFlushNotRequired` condition is misleading.** It is reported on every Keystone CR that omits `spec.trustFlush`, and the user-visible message is *\"Trust flush is not configured\"*. That phrasing reads as a positive signal (\"the operator says everything is fine\") when in fact the cluster is running with an unbounded-growth `trust` table. The condition is not actionable — there is no error to surface — so any operator who reads it interprets it as \"no action needed.\" Flipping the default removes the misleading condition from the happy path entirely; it remains as a legacy bypass-only signal that is never seen in production.

**Affected Areas**:
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:80-132` — add `if obj.Spec.TrustFlush == nil { obj.Spec.TrustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{Schedule: \"0 * * * *\", Suspend: false} }` block alongside the existing `Replicas`/`AdminUser`/`Cache.Backend` defaults
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:75-79,335-339` — godoc rewrite reflecting the default-on posture
- `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook_test.go` — add `TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` next to the existing `TestDefault_*` cases
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush.go:34-47` — reframe the nil-branch as legacy-bypass-only with a warning log; keep `deleteCronJob()` for finalizer paths
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush_test.go:168-230` — annotate the `TrustFlushNil_*` cases as bypass-path tests
- `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go`, `integration_test.go` — adjust any assertion that runs through the webhook and expects `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` to expect `reason: TrustFlushReady`
- `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml:12` — change `trustFlush: null` to `trustFlush: {suspend: true}` and update the matching `chainsaw-test.yaml:136+` assertions
- `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/` (new) — fixture + chainsaw test for the omitted-`trustFlush` path
- `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:144,461-475` — narrative + table edits
- `docs/quick-start.md:589`, `docs/guides/observability.md:60`, `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md:192`, `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:280,2406-2418,2628`, `docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md:64,842-845` — wording updates and the new `trust-flush-default` suite reference
- `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` — upgrade callout for clusters with never-flushed trust tables

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] `KeystoneWebhook.Default()` materializes `obj.Spec.TrustFlush` to `&TrustFlushSpec{Schedule: \"0 * * * *\", Suspend: false}` when the pointer is nil — verified by a focused unit test
- [ ] Applying a Keystone CR that omits `spec.trustFlush` results in (a) `kubectl get keystone -o yaml` showing a populated `spec.trustFlush:` block, (b) a CronJob `{name}-trust-flush` with `schedule: \"0 * * * *\"` and `suspend: false`, and (c) `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady`
- [ ] Setting `spec.trustFlush.suspend: true` on an existing CR pauses the CronJob (`spec.suspend == true`) without deleting it; `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` is preserved
- [ ] Patching `spec.trustFlush: null` on a webhook-enabled cluster causes admission to re-default the pointer back to a populated struct — the user cannot unset the field
- [ ] On a cluster bypassing the defaulting webhook (envtest without the defaulter wired up, or `--validate=false` paths), the reconciler falls back to the legacy nil-branch, deletes any existing CronJob, and emits `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` plus a controller-log warning
- [ ] The new `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default` chainsaw suite passes on a kind cluster, asserting the omitted-field happy path end-to-end
- [ ] The existing `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush` chainsaw suite still passes, with the disable-path step rewritten to use `suspend: true`
- [ ] All controller-package unit tests pass, including the relabelled bypass-path cases in `reconcile_trustflush_test.go`
- [ ] `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` documents the new default and the `suspend: true` opt-out; `docs/quick-start.md` no longer suggests trust flush is opt-in; `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` includes the upgrade callout
- [ ] On a brownfield cluster upgraded from the previous operator version, a Keystone CR that previously omitted `spec.trustFlush` ends up with `spec.trustFlush` materialized, a CronJob created within one reconcile, and the first trust-flush Job fires within the hourly window — verified manually on a kind upgrade walkthrough or via a follow-up upgrade-fixture chainsaw test

**Non-Goals**:
- Adding a new `Disabled bool` field to `TrustFlushSpec`. The existing `Suspend` field already covers the *\"keep the CronJob, do not run\"* use case, and a `Disabled` field would duplicate that without a clean delete path. If a user genuinely wants no CronJob at all, the answer is *\"this operator owns trust flush; you cannot opt out\"* — same posture as fernet rotation.
- Changing the field shape from `*TrustFlushSpec` to a non-pointer `TrustFlushSpec`. That would be the *cleanest* CRD-level solution (the kubebuilder leaf defaults would fire automatically on materialized empty structs), but it is a deeper API change with knock-on effects on existing clients and on the deepcopy generation. The webhook-Default approach achieves the same observable behavior with a single-line code change and no API-shape break.
- Backporting the new default to older `keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1` clients. The `v1alpha1` API is still pre-stable and operators are expected to track operator releases; a one-line upgrade-flow callout is sufficient.
- Auto-running an initial bulk `keystone-manage trust_flush --date <past>` on the first reconcile after upgrade to drain a backlog. That is an operator-initiated decision (it can be expensive on a never-flushed cluster) and remains documented in the upgrade flow rather than encoded in the controller.
- Surfacing trust-flush job-failure metrics or alerting beyond the existing `TrustFlushReady` condition. Job-level observability is a separate concern (out of scope; would belong to a future *\"surface CronJob outcomes on the CR status\"* enhancement).

**References**:
- CC-0057 — original trust-flush feature (introduced `*TrustFlushSpec` and the `TrustFlushReady`/`TrustFlushNotRequired` conditions)
- `KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush` field: `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:75-79`
- `TrustFlushSpec` struct with leaf defaults: `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go:335-351`
- Reconciler nil-branch: `operators/keystone/internal/controller/reconcile_trustflush.go:34-47`
- Defaulting webhook anchor: `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_webhook.go:76-132`
- Existing prior art for *\"materialize an empty struct so leaf defaults fire\"* — `Replicas = 3` and `Bootstrap.Region = \"RegionOne\"` defaulting at `keystone_webhook.go:81-98`
- Conceptual symmetry: `CredentialKeysSpec` non-pointer with leaf defaults at `keystone_types.go:73,323-333`
- Existing trust-flush e2e suite: `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/`

**Labels:** enhancement

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/286

stories.title: Cluster operator gets trust flush enabled by default on a freshly bootstrapped Keystone CR (CC-0096)
stories.want: a Keystone CR that omits `spec.trustFlush` to still purge expired trust delegations on a sane hourly schedule
stories.so_that: the `trust` table does not grow unbounded for the lifetime of the deployment and I do not have to remember an opt-in field
criteria: Applying a Keystone CR without `spec.trustFlush` results in `kubectl get keystone -o yaml` showing a populated `spec.trustFlush:` block with `schedule: "0 * * * *"` and `suspend: false`
A CronJob named `{name}-trust-flush` is created in the same namespace with `spec.schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `spec.suspend == false`
`status.conditions[type=TrustFlushReady]` is `True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` and `message: "Trust flush CronJob is configured"`
No new fields are introduced on the CRD; the change is purely a defaulting-webhook flip plus reconciler reframing

stories.title: Cluster operator pauses trust flush without removing the CronJob (CC-0096)
stories.want: a way to temporarily stop the trust flush schedule without deleting the CronJob resource
stories.so_that: I can pause the housekeeping during a maintenance window or large migration and resume by toggling one field
criteria: Setting `spec.trustFlush.suspend: true` on an existing CR results in the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob having `spec.suspend == true`
The CronJob resource is NOT deleted while suspended
`TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` is preserved while suspended (no new `TrustFlushSuspended` reason)
Setting `spec.trustFlush.suspend: false` again resumes the schedule and Jobs fire on the next cron tick

stories.title: Cluster operator cannot accidentally unset trust flush back to nil on a webhook-enabled cluster (CC-0096)
stories.want: an API contract where `spec.trustFlush: null` is re-defaulted on admission
stories.so_that: I cannot disable trust flush by mistake (e.g. via `kubectl patch`) on a production cluster that needs the housekeeping
criteria: Patching `spec.trustFlush: null` on a webhook-enabled cluster does NOT remove the CronJob — the next `kubectl get keystone -o yaml` shows the materialized `trustFlush:` block again
The `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob remains present with the documented hourly schedule
`TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` is reported after the round-trip
Documentation explicitly states that there is no nil-back path — admission re-defaults the pointer

stories.title: Operator developer keeps the legacy bypass branch alive for envtest and webhook-less clusters (CC-0096)
stories.want: the reconciler to handle `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` defensively even after the webhook flip
stories.so_that: envtest unit tests that construct CRs by hand, and clusters running the controller without the defaulting webhook (e.g. `--validate=false`), do not panic and continue to behave deterministically
criteria: When the reconciler observes `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil`, it logs a warning naming the CR (namespace/name) and identifying the bypass posture
The reconciler still calls `deleteCronJob()` for the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob (idempotent NotFound handling)
It sets `TrustFlushReady=True` with the legacy `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` and `message` documenting the bypass
No production reconcile path can reach the bypass branch when the defaulting webhook is wired up

stories.title: Brownfield operator upgrading from the previous operator version sees trust flush enabled without a re-apply (CC-0096)
stories.want: existing Keystone CRs that previously omitted `spec.trustFlush` to start running trust flush after the operator upgrade
stories.so_that: I get the new default-on behavior without having to edit every CR by hand
criteria: After upgrading the operator, a reconcile that round-trips an old CR through the API server materializes `spec.trustFlush` to the populated default
On the next reconcile after that, the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is created with `schedule: "0 * * * *"`
Within the configured interval, the first `keystone-manage trust_flush` Job fires successfully

`docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` calls out the brownfield behavior and includes a one-line warning that the first Job can be expensive on never-flushed clusters with a multi-million-row `trust` table

stories.title: Documentation reader understands that trust flush is now default-on (CC-0096)
stories.want: all CRD reference, quick-start, observability, advanced-configuration, and reconciler docs to reflect default-on trust flush and the `suspend: true` opt-out
stories.so_that: I do not believe I have to opt in or that the absence of trust flush is a normal happy-path signal
criteria: `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:144,461-475` describes `spec.trustFlush` as defaulting to a populated struct (hourly schedule), documents `suspend: true` as the documented pause path, and notes there is no nil-back path
`docs/quick-start.md:589` and `docs/guides/observability.md:60` no longer suggest trust flush is opt-in
`docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md:192` notes the default-on posture and links to `suspend: true`
`docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md:280,2406-2418,2628` describes the Path 2 nil-branch as legacy-bypass-only
`docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md:64,842-845` references the new `trust-flush-default` chainsaw suite

stories.title: QA / CI validates default-on trust flush end-to-end on a kind cluster (CC-0096)
stories.want: a chainsaw test suite that asserts the omitted-`spec.trustFlush` happy path on a real kind cluster
stories.so_that: regressions to the default-on behavior are caught in CI and not in production
criteria: A new chainsaw suite at `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/` applies a Keystone CR that does NOT include `spec.trustFlush`

The suite asserts the Keystone CR's serialized spec contains a populated `trustFlush:` block, the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob exists with `schedule: "0 * * * *"` and `suspend: false`, and `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady`

The existing `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/` suite still passes, with the disable-path step rewritten to use `suspend: true` (CronJob preserved with `spec.suspend == true`, `reason: TrustFlushReady` preserved)
Both suites are referenced in `docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md`

stories.title: Operator developer keeps unit tests aligned with the webhook flip (CC-0096)
stories.want: unit tests in the controller and webhook packages updated to reflect the new default-on posture
stories.so_that: test signals match the production behavior and the legacy bypass branch is explicitly labelled
criteria: A new `TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` in `keystone_webhook_test.go` asserts that after `Default()` the pointer is non-nil with `Schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `Suspend == false`
Existing `TrustFlushNil_*` cases in `reconcile_trustflush_test.go:168-230` are annotated with a comment noting they exercise the bypass branch (not a production code path)

Any controller test that runs through the webhook and previously asserted `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` is updated to expect `reason: TrustFlushReady` (audit `keystone_controller_test.go` and `integration_test.go`)
All tests in `operators/keystone/...` pass under `go test ./...`

requirements.description: The Keystone defaulting webhook SHALL materialize `spec.trustFlush` to a populated `TrustFlushSpec` when the pointer is nil, with `Schedule = "0 * * * *"` and `Suspend = false`.
requirements.rationale: Closes the gap where leaf-level kubebuilder defaults on `Schedule` and `Suspend` are dead code for CRs that omit the parent field; mirrors the established `Replicas = 3` / `Bootstrap.Region = "RegionOne"` defaulting pattern.

scenarios.name: Nil pointer is materialized on create
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with `spec.trustFlush == nil` is admitted through `KeystoneWebhook.Default()`
scenarios.then: after `Default()` returns, `obj.Spec.TrustFlush` is non-nil with `Schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `Suspend == false`
and_then: no other field on `KeystoneSpec` is mutated by the trust-flush block
the existing `+kubebuilder:default` markers on `Schedule` and `Suspend` remain in place as defense-in-depth

scenarios.name: Existing user-set TrustFlush is preserved
scenarios.when: a Keystone CR with `spec.trustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{Schedule: "*/30 * * * *", Suspend: true, Args: ["--date", "2026-01-01"]}` is admitted
scenarios.then: after `Default()`, `obj.Spec.TrustFlush.Schedule` remains `"*/30 * * * *"`, `Suspend` remains `true`, and `Args` remains `["--date", "2026-01-01"]`
and_then: the webhook never overwrites a user-supplied value

scenarios.name: Re-default on patch-to-null
scenarios.when: a user patches `spec.trustFlush: null` on a webhook-enabled cluster
scenarios.then: the admission round-trip re-runs `Default()` and the persisted object has a populated `spec.trustFlush` again
and_then: the user observes the materialized struct on the next `kubectl get keystone -o yaml`
the existing `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is preserved (no delete/recreate churn)

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL treat `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` as a legacy bypass branch — log a warning, delete any pre-existing `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob, and set `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired`.
requirements.rationale: Defends against envtest fixtures and webhook-less clusters that bypass the defaulting webhook; preserves backwards-compatible signal strings already referenced by tests and chainsaw fixtures.

scenarios.name: Bypass branch logs a warning and deletes any existing CronJob
scenarios.when: `reconcileTrustFlush()` is invoked with `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` and an existing `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is present
scenarios.then: the reconciler logs a warning naming the CR (namespace/name) and identifying the bypass posture, calls `deleteCronJob()`, and the CronJob is deleted
and_then: `TrustFlushReady=True` is set with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired`
the condition `ObservedGeneration` matches `keystone.Generation`

scenarios.name: Bypass branch is idempotent when no CronJob exists
scenarios.when: `reconcileTrustFlush()` is invoked with `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` and no `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob exists
scenarios.then: the reconciler logs the warning, `deleteCronJob()` returns nil (NotFound is ignored), and `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` is set
and_then: no error is returned
no event is fired

scenarios.name: Delete error is propagated
scenarios.when: `reconcileTrustFlush()` is invoked with `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush == nil` and the underlying `Delete` call fails with a non-NotFound error
scenarios.then: the error is returned wrapped with `"deleting trust flush CronJob"` context
and_then: no condition is set on this reconcile pass
the controller-runtime exponential backoff retries the reconcile

requirements.description: The reconciler SHALL create or update the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob and set `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` whenever `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush` is non-nil — including the freshly-defaulted case where the user did not set the field.
requirements.rationale: Existing Path 1 of `reconcileTrustFlush()` is unchanged in behavior, but it now fires for every webhook-defaulted CR; the condition reason must be `TrustFlushReady` even when the schedule was webhook-injected.

scenarios.name: Webhook-defaulted CR creates CronJob with hourly schedule
scenarios.when: `reconcileTrustFlush()` is invoked with `keystone.Spec.TrustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{Schedule: "0 * * * *", Suspend: false}` (webhook default)
scenarios.then: the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is created via `job.EnsureCronJob` with `spec.schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `spec.suspend == false`
and_then: `TrustFlushReady=True` is set with `reason: TrustFlushReady` and `message: "Trust flush CronJob is configured"`
`ObservedGeneration` matches `keystone.Generation`
the CronJob has an OwnerReference back to the Keystone CR with `controller: true`

scenarios.name: Suspend toggle preserves the CronJob and the condition reason
scenarios.when: an existing CR is updated to `spec.trustFlush.suspend = true`
scenarios.then: `reconcileTrustFlush()` updates the CronJob via `job.EnsureCronJob` so that `spec.suspend == true` while keeping the same name and ownerReferences
and_then: `TrustFlushReady=True` is preserved with `reason: TrustFlushReady` (NOT `TrustFlushSuspended`)
no new condition reason is introduced

scenarios.name: Schedule change updates the CronJob in place
scenarios.when: an existing CR is updated to `spec.trustFlush.schedule = "*/30 * * * *"`
scenarios.then: the next reconcile updates the existing `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob via `job.EnsureCronJob` so that `spec.schedule == "*/30 * * * *"`
and_then: the CronJob `metadata.name` and ownerReferences are preserved (no delete/recreate)
`TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` is preserved

requirements.description: The Keystone API godoc on `KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush` and `TrustFlushSpec` SHALL describe the new default-on posture, the `suspend: true` opt-out, and the absence of a nil-back path.
requirements.rationale: The godoc is exposed via `kubectl explain` and rendered into the CRD reference doc; misleading wording (`*"When set..."*`) is exactly the kind of footgun this feature is removing.

scenarios.name: Field godoc reflects default-on posture
scenarios.when: a reader runs `kubectl explain keystone.spec.trustFlush` on a cluster with the new operator
scenarios.then: the description states that the defaulting webhook materializes a populated TrustFlushSpec when the field is unset, that `suspend: true` is the documented pause path, and that admission re-defaults the pointer if the user patches it to null
and_then: no claim of opt-in semantics remains in the godoc

scenarios.name: Struct godoc reflects materialization
scenarios.when: a reader runs `kubectl explain keystone.spec.trustFlush.schedule` (or reads the generated zz_generated.deepcopy.go)
scenarios.then: the `TrustFlushSpec` godoc explains that the parent struct is materialized by the webhook so that the leaf `+kubebuilder:default` markers fire deterministically

scenarios.name: Non-affected godoc is unchanged
scenarios.when: the change is reviewed
scenarios.then: godoc on unrelated fields (`Schedule`, `Suspend`, `Args`) only changes if necessary to reflect the new posture
and_then: no scope creep into other `KeystoneSpec` fields

requirements.description: The webhook validation SHALL continue to validate `spec.trustFlush.schedule` as a cron expression, and the validation MUST accept the webhook-defaulted value `"0 * * * *"` without error.
requirements.rationale: Ensures admission stays consistent: the existing `validate()` cron parsing at `keystone_webhook.go:240-253` runs after `Default()`, and the materialized default must pass `cron.ParseStandard`.

scenarios.name: Webhook-defaulted schedule passes validation
scenarios.when: `KeystoneWebhook.ValidateCreate()` runs after `Default()` materialized `spec.trustFlush` with `Schedule = "0 * * * *"`
scenarios.then: validation passes without raising an error on `spec.trustFlush.schedule`
and_then: the cron expression is accepted by `cron.ParseStandard`

scenarios.name: Invalid user-supplied cron is still rejected
scenarios.when: a user submits a Keystone CR with `spec.trustFlush.schedule = "not-a-cron"`
scenarios.then: validation rejects with `field.Invalid` on `spec.trustFlush.schedule` carrying the parse error
and_then: the existing error wording is preserved

scenarios.name: Empty schedule still rejected on bypass path
scenarios.when: validation is invoked on a CR where `spec.trustFlush != nil` but `spec.trustFlush.schedule == ""` (e.g., webhook bypass)
scenarios.then: validation rejects with `field.Required` on `spec.trustFlush.schedule`

requirements.description: The existing trust-flush e2e suite SHALL continue to pass after the disable-path step is rewritten from `trustFlush: null` to `trustFlush: {suspend: true}`, and the assertions SHALL expect `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady` plus a CronJob with `spec.suspend == true`.
requirements.rationale: The user-facing `suspend: true` semantics are the documented pause path; the existing chainsaw fixture must reflect production behavior on a webhook-enabled cluster.

scenarios.name: Suspend path preserves the CronJob
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test patches `spec.trustFlush` to `{suspend: true}` on the running CR
scenarios.then: the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob is preserved (not deleted) with `spec.suspend == true`
and_then: no new Job pods are created during the assert window

scenarios.name: Suspend path preserves TrustFlushReady reason
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test asserts on `status.conditions[type=TrustFlushReady]` after the suspend patch
scenarios.then: the condition is `True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady`
and_then: the test header documents the rationale (suspend != not-required)

scenarios.name: Existing pre-suspend assertions are unchanged
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test runs steps 1-6 (apply CR, assert Ready, assert TrustFlushReady, assert CronJob, run manual flush, assert Ready maintained)
scenarios.then: all of those steps pass without change to fixtures or assertions

requirements.description: A new `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/` chainsaw suite SHALL apply a Keystone CR that omits `spec.trustFlush` and assert end-to-end that the webhook materializes the field, the CronJob is created with the hourly schedule, and the condition reports `TrustFlushReady`.
requirements.rationale: Without a dedicated e2e suite, the default-on behavior is only covered at the unit level; chainsaw is the only place to verify that the API server returns the materialized spec on `kubectl get`.

scenarios.name: Materialized spec round-trips through the API server
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test applies the new fixture (no `spec.trustFlush` block) and queries the persisted Keystone CR
scenarios.then: `status.conditions[type=Ready].status == "True"` with `reason: AllReady`, and the spec returned by the API server contains a populated `trustFlush:` block with `schedule: "0 * * * *"` and `suspend: false`
and_then: the test uses jmespath/jp expressions to read `spec.trustFlush.schedule` and assert equality

scenarios.name: CronJob is created with the hourly default
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test asserts on `CronJob/{name}-trust-flush`
scenarios.then: the CronJob exists with `spec.schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `spec.suspend == false`
and_then: the CronJob's ownerReferences point back to the Keystone CR

scenarios.name: Condition reports TrustFlushReady (not TrustFlushNotRequired)
scenarios.when: the chainsaw test asserts on `status.conditions[type=TrustFlushReady]`
scenarios.then: the condition is `True` with `reason: TrustFlushReady`
and_then: the test does NOT pass if `reason` is `TrustFlushNotRequired`

requirements.description: The reference documentation (`docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md`) and the user-facing guides (`docs/quick-start.md`, `docs/guides/observability.md`, `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md`) SHALL be updated to describe trust flush as default-on with `suspend: true` as the documented pause path.
requirements.rationale: Misleading docs claiming opt-in semantics are the third motivation driver; the docs must match the new behavior or operators will not discover the change.

scenarios.name: CRD reference describes the new default
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` at the `trustFlush` row (line 144) and the `TrustFlushSpec` section (lines 461-475)
scenarios.then: the wording reflects the populated default, the `suspend: true` opt-out, and the no-nil-back-path contract
and_then: the CronJob mapping table at lines 480-491 is updated to note the materialized-via-webhook source

scenarios.name: Reconciler reference reframes Path 2 as legacy bypass
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` at the trust-flush sub-reconciler section (lines 280, 2406-2418, 2628)
scenarios.then: the Path 1 / Path 2 split is described as legacy-bypass-only for Path 2, with the warning-log behavior documented
and_then: the condition table at line 2451 is updated to mark `TrustFlushNotRequired` as bypass-only

scenarios.name: Quick-start and guides drop opt-in language
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/quick-start.md:589`, `docs/guides/observability.md:60`, and `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md:192`
scenarios.then: no row claims trust flush is conditional on `spec.trustFlush` being set; the wording reflects the hourly default and links to `suspend: true` as the pause path

requirements.description: The upgrade-flow documentation (`docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md`) SHALL include a brownfield callout explaining that a Keystone CR which previously omitted `spec.trustFlush` will get the field materialized on the next admission round-trip after the operator upgrade, that the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob will be created on the next reconcile, and that the first Job can be expensive on never-flushed clusters with multi-million-row `trust` tables.
requirements.rationale: Operators upgrading from CC-0057 to CC-0096 need to know about the silent behavioral change; without the callout, the first Job on a long-lived cluster could OOM or pin the DB.

scenarios.name: Upgrade callout describes the materialization trigger
scenarios.when: a reader opens `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` at the new CC-0096 section
scenarios.then: the section explains that any reconcile round-trip through the API server (including a user-issued `kubectl apply`/`kubectl edit`) will materialize `spec.trustFlush`

scenarios.name: Upgrade callout warns about expensive first Jobs
scenarios.when: a reader opens the same section
scenarios.then: the section recommends running `keystone-manage trust_flush --date <recent>` manually before the upgrade, or staggering the rollout, on clusters where the trust table is known to be very large

scenarios.name: Upgrade callout is cross-linked
scenarios.when: a reader follows the trust-flush row in `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`
scenarios.then: a hyperlink points to the upgrade callout in `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md`

requirements.description: Existing controller and integration tests that previously asserted `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` on a webhook-enabled path SHALL be updated to expect `reason: TrustFlushReady`, while bypass-path tests SHALL be annotated with a comment identifying them as legacy bypass tests.
requirements.rationale: Without the audit, the test suite produces false-green signal: bypass-path tests would pass even though the production webhook path now reports the opposite reason.

scenarios.name: Bypass-path unit tests are explicitly labelled
scenarios.when: a developer reads `reconcile_trustflush_test.go` (lines 168-230)
scenarios.then: the `TestReconcileTrustFlush_TrustFlushNil_*` tests have a comment block stating they exercise the legacy bypass path that production CRs will not reach
and_then: the comment cites CC-0096 by feature ID

scenarios.name: Webhook-enabled integration tests expect TrustFlushReady
scenarios.when: a developer runs `go test ./operators/keystone/internal/controller/...` after the webhook flip
scenarios.then: tests in `integration_test.go` and `keystone_controller_test.go` that round-trip through the defaulting webhook expect `reason: TrustFlushReady` on the happy path
and_then: the previously omitted-trustFlush integration test (`TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedDoesNotCreateCronJob` at integration_test.go:1753) is either renamed to reflect the bypass posture or rewritten to assert the materialized happy path

scenarios.name: Webhook unit test covers the defaulting block
scenarios.when: a developer runs `go test ./operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/...`
scenarios.then: `TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` passes, asserting `obj.Spec.TrustFlush.Schedule == "0 * * * *"` and `Suspend == false` after `Default()`
and_then: an existing-value preservation test verifies `Default()` does not overwrite a user-supplied `TrustFlushSpec`

Add `TestReconcileTrustFlush_WebhookDefaulted_CreatesCronJobAndReportsReady` to `reconcile_trustflush_test.go` simulating the post-webhook state (`Spec.TrustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{Schedule: "0 * * * *", Suspend: false}`) and asserting CronJob creation plus `reason: TrustFlushReady`; cite CC-0096, REQ-003 (REQ-003)

Add `TestReconcileTrustFlush_SuspendTrue_PreservesCronJobAndReason` to `reconcile_trustflush_test.go` asserting that `Spec.TrustFlush.Suspend = true` produces a CronJob with `spec.suspend == true` and preserves `reason: TrustFlushReady`; cite CC-0096, REQ-003 (REQ-003)

Audit `operators/keystone/internal/controller/keystone_controller_test.go` for `TrustFlushReady` assertions (lines 354, 462, 479, 1178, 1199, 1230); confirm they continue to pass under the webhook-defaulted posture or update them to expect `reason: TrustFlushReady` where they previously expected `TrustFlushNotRequired`; cite CC-0096, REQ-010 (REQ-010)

Replace `TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedDoesNotCreateCronJob` at `integration_test.go:1753` with `TestIntegration_TrustFlush_OmittedFieldMaterializedAndCronJobCreated`: apply CR through the envtest defaulting webhook, assert the persisted CR shows `spec.trustFlush` populated, the CronJob is created, and `reason: TrustFlushReady`; cite CC-0096, REQ-001 (REQ-001)

Update the disable-path block in `integration_test.go:1735-1750` so it patches `spec.trustFlush.suspend: true` instead of removing the field, and asserts CronJob preservation with `spec.suspend == true` plus `reason: TrustFlushReady`; rename to `TestIntegration_TrustFlush_SuspendTruePreservesCronJob`; cite CC-0096, REQ-006 (REQ-006)

Add `TestIntegration_TrustFlush_PatchToNullReDefaultsAndPreservesCronJob` to `integration_test.go` asserting that `kubectl patch ... Rewrite `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml:12` to patch `spec.trustFlush: {suspend: true}` instead of `null`; update the file header comment to cite CC-0096 alongside CC-0057 (REQ-006)

Update `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush/chainsaw-test.yaml` step 7-9 (lines 136-162) to assert the suspend-path: `(conditions[?type == 'TrustFlushReady'])` reports `reason: TrustFlushReady`, the `{name}-trust-flush` CronJob persists with `spec.suspend: true`, and no recent Job pods exist; refresh the test header to document the new posture and cite CC-0096 (REQ-006)

Create `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/00-keystone-cr.yaml` mirroring the existing `trust-flush/00-keystone-cr.yaml` but OMITTING the `trustFlush:` block entirely; use distinct CR name (e.g. `keystone-trust-flush-default`) to avoid collisions with the sibling suite; cite CC-0096 (REQ-007)

Update `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md:144` row description for `trustFlush` to reflect default-on posture, `suspend: true` opt-out, and no nil-back path; cite CC-0096 in the inline change rationale (REQ-008)

Update `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` `TrustFlushSpec` section narrative at lines 461-475 (replace the `nil` Path-2 wording with the materialized-by-default description); update the CronJob mapping table at lines 480-491 to note the materialized-via-webhook source; cite CC-0096 (REQ-008)
Update `docs/quick-start.md:589`: change `"Trust-flush CronJob created (if spec.trustFlush is set)"` to `"Trust-flush CronJob created — defaults to hourly"`; cite CC-0096 in commit message (REQ-008)
Update `docs/guides/observability.md:60` the same way as quick-start; cite CC-0096 (REQ-008)
Update `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md:192` to note default-on posture and link to `suspend: true` as the documented pause path; cite CC-0096 (REQ-008)

Update `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md` lines 280, 2392-2418, 2451 and 2628 to describe Path 2 as legacy-bypass-only; document the warning-log behavior; mark `TrustFlushNotRequired` as bypass-only in the condition table; cite CC-0096 (REQ-008)

Update `docs/reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md` lines 64 and 842-845 to add the new `trust-flush-default/` suite to the directory tree and the file table, and to update the `01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml` description to reflect `suspend: true`; cite CC-0096 (REQ-008)

Add a new CC-0096 section to `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` describing: (a) how brownfield CRs that omit `spec.trustFlush` get the field materialized on the next admission round-trip after operator upgrade, (b) the resulting CronJob creation timeline, (c) the recommendation to run `keystone-manage trust_flush --date <recent>` before upgrade or stagger rollout on clusters with very large trust tables; cross-link from `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md` (REQ-009)

test_specifications.story: Operator developer keeps unit tests aligned with the webhook flip (CC-0096)
test_specifications.expected: [unit] `Default()` does not overwrite a user-supplied `TrustFlushSpec` (Schedule, Suspend, and Args remain as set by the caller)

test_specifications.story: Operator developer keeps unit tests aligned with the webhook flip (CC-0096)
test_specifications.expected: [unit] After `Default()` materializes `spec.trustFlush` with `Schedule = "0 * * * *"`, `ValidateCreate()` returns no error on the trust-flush field

test_specifications.expected: [unit] (Re-purpose of existing `TestReconcileTrustFlush_TrustFlushNil_ExistingCronJob_DeletesCronJob` at reconcile_trustflush_test.go:185 with bypass-path comment annotation) — pre-existing CronJob is deleted, `TrustFlushReady=True` with `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired`, no error returned

test_specifications.story: Operator developer keeps the legacy bypass branch alive for envtest and webhook-less clusters (CC-0096)
test_specifications.expected: [unit] (Existing `TestReconcileTrustFlush_DeleteError_Propagated` at reconcile_trustflush_test.go:283, annotated as bypass-path) — when the underlying `Delete` call errors with a non-NotFound error, the wrapped error is returned with `"deleting trust flush CronJob"` context

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator gets trust flush enabled by default on a freshly bootstrapped Keystone CR (CC-0096)

test_specifications.story: Cluster operator cannot accidentally unset trust flush back to nil on a webhook-enabled cluster (CC-0096)

review_criteria: BLOCKER: `KeystoneWebhook.Default()` materializes `Spec.TrustFlush` to the documented default when nil; existing user-set values are preserved (verified by `TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_MaterializesDefaultSpec` and `TestDefault_TrustFlushSet_PreservesExplicitValues`)

BLOCKER: The `reconcile_trustflush.go` nil-branch is reframed as legacy bypass with a warning log, retains `deleteCronJob()` and the `reason: TrustFlushNotRequired` for backwards compatibility, and is unreachable on the production webhook path

BLOCKER: All unit, integration, and chainsaw tests pass under `go test ./operators/keystone/...` and the chainsaw kind-cluster run; the new `tests/e2e/keystone/trust-flush-default/` suite asserts the materialized-spec round-trip and the CronJob creation

BLOCKER: `docs/reference/keystone-crd.md`, `docs/reference/keystone-reconciler.md`, `docs/quick-start.md`, `docs/guides/observability.md`, `docs/guides/advanced-configuration.md`, and `docs/reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md` all reflect the default-on posture; no doc still claims trust flush is opt-in
CRITICAL: The reconciler does not introduce a new `TrustFlushSuspended` reason; `suspend: true` keeps `reason: TrustFlushReady` (mirrors the autoscaling-suspended-HPA precedent)
CRITICAL: No CRD-shape change — `spec.trustFlush` remains a `*TrustFlushSpec` pointer; the `+kubebuilder:default` markers on `Schedule` and `Suspend` are preserved as defense-in-depth
CRITICAL: The webhook's `validate()` cron-parsing block at `keystone_webhook.go:240-253` is unchanged in semantics and still rejects empty / invalid cron expressions on the bypass path
CRITICAL: All docstring/comment/test references include the `CC-0096` feature ID (per project rule 11); no orphan references like "see related task"
MINOR: The bypass-path warning log is structured (`log.FromContext(ctx).Info`) with `"namespace"`/`"name"`/`"reason":"webhook-bypass"` keys so it is greppable and parseable

MINOR: Deferred items (no `Disabled` field, no API-shape change to non-pointer, no auto-bulk-flush, no metrics enhancement) are explicitly listed in the implementation notes — reviewers should reject any scope creep into these
MINOR: The new `trust-flush-default/` chainsaw suite uses a CR name distinct from the existing `trust-flush/` suite to allow both suites to run side-by-side without resource collisions
implementation_notes:
ARCHITECTURAL DECISIONS

(1) Defaulting via webhook, NOT via API-shape change. The cleanest CRD-level fix would be to drop the pointer and make `TrustFlush` a non-pointer `TrustFlushSpec` so the leaf `+kubebuilder:default` markers fire automatically (mirroring `CredentialKeysSpec` at `keystone_types.go:73,323-333`). That option is explicitly out of scope (Non-Goals): it is a deeper API-shape change with knock-on effects on existing clients and on `zz_generated.deepcopy.go`. The chosen webhook-Default approach achieves the same observable behavior with a single-line code change and zero CRD-shape break.

(2) Path 2 is preserved as a legacy bypass branch, NOT deleted. Three reasons: (a) envtest fixtures construct CRs by hand and bypass the defaulting webhook — deleting Path 2 would crash on `nil`; (b) clusters running with `--validate=false` or a misconfigured webhook bypass admission too; (c) the existing constant strings `TrustFlushNotRequired` are referenced by tests and chainsaw fixtures, and ripping them out would force a churn of unrelated assertions. The trade-off: one inert branch in the reconciler that emits a warning log when reached. Acceptable.

(3) `suspend: true` keeps `reason: TrustFlushReady`. This mirrors the established convention that an Autoscaling-suspended HPA still reports `AutoscalingReady`. Introducing a `TrustFlushSuspended` reason would force every consumer of the condition (chainsaw assertions, dashboards, `kubectl get -o jsonpath` chains) to update for a state that is already represented in `spec.suspend`.

(4) Webhook is the single source of truth. We set `Schedule = "0 * * * *"` and `Suspend = false` explicitly in `Default()` rather than trusting the leaf `+kubebuilder:default` markers to fire in the same admission round-trip. The kubebuilder leaf markers stay in place as defense-in-depth for callers that bypass the webhook (envtest without the defaulter wired up).

DATA FLOW

```
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  User: kubectl apply / kubectl edit / kubectl patch         │
        └─────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  v
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  API server: schema validation + +kubebuilder:default        │
        │  markers fire on materialized leaf fields                    │
        └─────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  v
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  Mutating webhook: KeystoneWebhook.Default()                │
        │   if obj.Spec.TrustFlush == nil:                            │
        │       obj.Spec.TrustFlush = &TrustFlushSpec{                │
        │           Schedule: "0 * * * *", Suspend: false}            │
        │   ← NEW BLOCK (CC-0096, REQ-001)                            │
        └─────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │
                                  v
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  Validating webhook: KeystoneWebhook.validate()              │
        │  (unchanged — cron.ParseStandard accepts the default)        │
        └─────────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │  persist to etcd
                                  v
        ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │  KeystoneReconciler.reconcileTrustFlush                      │
        │   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐     │
        │   │ if Spec.TrustFlush == nil (BYPASS, log warning):  │     │
        │   │     deleteCronJob() ; reason=TrustFlushNotRequired│     │
        │   │ else (PRODUCTION):                                 │     │
        │   │     EnsureCronJob()  ; reason=TrustFlushReady     │     │
        │   └────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘     │
        └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

FILE-LEVEL ARCHITECTURE

```
  operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/
    ├── keystone_webhook.go          ← +1 defaulting block (CC-0096, REQ-001)
    ├── keystone_webhook_test.go     ← +TestDefault_TrustFlushNil_*, +TestDefault_TrustFlushSet_Preserves
    ├── keystone_types.go            ← godoc rewrite on TrustFlush field + TrustFlushSpec struct
  operators/keystone/internal/controller/
    ├── reconcile_trustflush.go      ← reframe Path 2 as legacy bypass, add warning log
    ├── reconcile_trustflush_test.go ← annotate bypass tests, +Webhook-defaulted tests
    ├── integration_test.go          ← rewrite OmittedDoesNotCreate → MaterializedAndCronJobCreated;
                                       rewrite disable-path → SuspendTruePreservesCronJob;
                                       +PatchToNullReDefaults
    ├── keystone_controller_test.go  ← audit TrustFlushReady assertions
  tests/e2e/keystone/
    ├── trust-flush/
    │   ├── 01-patch-disable-trust-flush.yaml ← null → {suspend: true}
    │   ├── chainsaw-test.yaml                ← rewrite step 7-9 assertions
    └── trust-flush-default/                  ← NEW SUITE
        ├── 00-keystone-cr.yaml               ← omits spec.trustFlush
        └── chainsaw-test.yaml
  docs/
    ├── reference/keystone-crd.md             ← row + section + table edits
    ├── reference/keystone-reconciler.md      ← Path 2 as legacy bypass
    ├── reference/keystone-e2e-tests.md       ← +trust-flush-default reference
    ├── reference/keystone-upgrade-flow.md    ← +CC-0096 brownfield section
    ├── quick-start.md                        ← line 589 wording
    ├── guides/observability.md               ← line 60 wording
    └── guides/advanced-configuration.md      ← line 192 wording
```

PITFALLS TO AVOID

(1) Do NOT delete the `+kubebuilder:default="0 * * * *"` and `+kubebuilder:default=false` markers on `TrustFlushSpec.Schedule` and `Suspend`. They serve as defense-in-depth for webhook-bypass paths.

(2) Do NOT use `+kubebuilder:default` on `KeystoneSpec.TrustFlush` itself. The pointer field cannot carry a struct default cleanly without invoking the `omitempty` round-trip footgun documented at `keystone_types.go:139-150` for the unrelated `TerminationGracePeriodSeconds` field. Use the webhook only.

(3) Do NOT introduce a `TrustFlushSuspended` condition reason. The condition vocabulary stays `{TrustFlushReady, TrustFlushNotRequired}`; `suspend: true` keeps `reason: TrustFlushReady` (see decision 3 above).

(4) Do NOT delete the `TrustFlushNil_*` reconciler tests. They now exercise the bypass branch, which still has a code path. Annotate them and rename them with a `Bypass` suffix instead.

(5) Do NOT change the chainsaw CR name in the existing `trust-flush/` suite. Use a distinct name (`keystone-trust-flush-default`) for the new suite so both can run side-by-side.

(6) Do NOT leave the deprecated `trustFlush: null` patch in the existing chainsaw fixture — on a webhook-enabled cluster the patch silently re-defaults, so the disable-path step would be a no-op assertion. Replace with `{suspend: true}` per task 3.1.

(7) Make sure the warning log on the bypass path is structured (`log.FromContext(ctx).Info` with `namespace`/`name`/`reason` keys) not `fmt.Printf`. The controller-runtime convention in this repo is `logr`-based.

DEFERRED ITEMS

- `Disabled bool` field on `TrustFlushSpec` — duplicate of `Suspend`; out of scope; revisit only if a delete-cronjob-but-keep-spec use case emerges.
- API shape change `*TrustFlushSpec` → `TrustFlushSpec` (non-pointer) — cleaner long-term solution; revisit at the next v1alpha1 → v1beta1 migration alongside other API-shape cleanups.
- Auto-running an initial `keystone-manage trust_flush --date <past>` Job on first reconcile after upgrade — operationally risky on never-flushed clusters; remains an operator-initiated decision documented in the upgrade flow.
- Surfacing trust-flush Job-failure metrics on `status` (e.g. `lastSuccessfulRunTime`, `lastFailedRunTime`) — separate concern; would belong to a future "surface CronJob outcomes on the CR status" enhancement.
- Backporting the new default to older `v1alpha1` clients — `v1alpha1` is pre-stable and operators are expected to track operator releases; the upgrade-flow callout (REQ-009) is sufficient.
